questions.question: Type the pin to configure — accepts CVM ball (e.g. H10), Verilog name (PEX_L4_RST_N), DT pin name (pex_l4_rst_n_pd1), or carrier signal name from the schematic (PCIE4_RST_L, CAM0_I2C_SCL, GPIO47). The skill will resolve via `modify_pinmux.py lookup` — if 0 matches, you'll see candidates; if >1, you'll be asked to disambiguate by DT pin name.

Per pin, choose the SFIO (Special Function I/O). Options are filtered to what the silicon supports on this pin (the `sfio_options` list returned by `lookup`). The (suggested) tag marks the SFIO matching `customer_usage` from the pinmux xlsm.
questions.guidance: Examples: spi5, i2c8, pe4_rst_l, pwm0, gpio. Picking `gpio` triggers an additional gpio-init entry in the gpio default-state DTSI on commit.

options.label: unused
options.description: park the pin tristated (enable-input=DISABLE, tristate=ENABLE) — recommended when the carrier doesn't route this signal

questions.question: Per pin, choose the initial state at boot.

options.label: n/a
options.description: not applicable — use for non-output pins where the level isn't a property of the pin
